what: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
text: Creates hazard object optionally fits via maximum likelihood. mirrors argument-oriented workflow legacy HAZARD C/SAS implementation: supply starting values theta function optimize produce fitted estimates.

title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
code: hazard(   formula = NULL,   data = NULL,   time = NULL,   status = NULL,   time_lower = NULL,   time_upper = NULL,   x = NULL,   time_windows = NULL,   theta = NULL,   dist = "weibull",   phases = NULL,   fit = FALSE,   weights = NULL,   control = list(),   ... )

title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
text: formula Optional formula form Surv(time, status) ~ predictors. provided, overrides direct time/status/x arguments extracts data. Example: hazard(Surv(time, status) ~ x1 + x2, data = df, dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE). data Optional data frame containing variables referenced formula. time Numeric follow-time vector. status Numeric logical event indicator vector. time_lower Optional numeric lower bound vector censoring intervals. Used status == 2 (interval-censored); defaults time NULL. time_upper Optional numeric upper bound vector censoring intervals. Used status %% c(-1, 2); defaults time NULL. x Optional design matrix (data frame coercible matrix). time_windows Optional numeric vector strictly positive cut points piecewise time-varying coefficients. provided, predictor column x expanded one column per time window window gets coefficient. theta Optional numeric coefficient vector (starting values optimization). dist Character baseline distribution label (default "weibull"). Use "multiphase" N-phase additive hazard models (requires phases). phases Optional named list hzr_phase() objects specifying phases multiphase model (dist = "multiphase").  See Examples. fit Logical; TRUE, fit model via maximum likelihood (default FALSE). weights Optional numeric vector observation weights (non-negative). observation's log-likelihood contribution multiplied weight. Use severity-weighted repeated events. Default NULL (unit weights). Implements SAS WEIGHT statement. control Named list control options (see Details). ... Additional named arguments retained parity legacy calling conventions.

title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
text: Control parameters: maxit: Maximum iterations (default 1000) reltol: Relative parameter change tolerance (default 1e-5) abstol: Absolute gradient norm tolerance (default 1e-6) method: Optimization method: "bfgs" "nm" (default "bfgs") condition: Condition number control (default 14) nocov, nocor: Suppress covariance/correlation output (legacy; -op M2) Censoring status coding: 1: Exact event time 0: Right-censored time -1: Left-censored upper bound time_upper \(time\) 2: Interval-censored interval \(time_lower, time_upper\) Time-varying coefficients: time_windows supplied, predictors expanded piecewise window interactions window coefficient vector. implemented design-matrix expansion, existing likelihood engines remain unchanged.

title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
text: Blackstone EH, Naftel DC, Turner Jr. decomposition time-varying hazard phases, incorporating separate stream concomitant information. J Stat Assoc. 1986;81(395):615–624. doi:10.1080/01621459.1986.10478314 Rajeswaran J, Blackstone EH, Ehrlinger J, Li L, Ishwaran H, Parides MK. Probability atrial fibrillation ablation: Using parametric nonlinear temporal decomposition mixed effects model. Stat Methods Med Res. 2018;27(1):126–141. doi:10.1177/0962280215623583

title: Build and optionally fit a hazard model — hazard
code: # -- Univariable Weibull ---------------------------------------------- set.seed(1) time   <- rexp(50, rate = 0.3) status <- sample(0:1, 50, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.3, 0.7)) fit <- hazard(time = time, status = status,               theta = c(0.3, 1.0), dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE) summary(fit) #> hazard model summary #>   observations: 50  #>   predictors:   0  #>   dist:         weibull  #>   engine:       native-r-m2  #>   converged:    TRUE  #>   log-lik:      -89.5173  #>   evaluations: fn=23, gr=7 #>  #> Coefficients: #>     estimate  std_error   z_stat      p_value #> mu 0.2190092 0.02728228 8.027524 9.945983e-16 #> nu 1.3389540 0.15829050 8.458840 2.700510e-17  # -- Formula interface with covariates -------------------------------- set.seed(1001) n   <- 180 dat <- data.frame(   time   = rexp(n, rate = 0.35) + 0.05,   status = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.6),   age    = rnorm(n, mean = 62, sd = 11),   nyha   = sample(1:4, n, replace = TRUE),   shock  = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.18) )  fit2 <- hazard(   survival::Surv(time, status) ~ age + nyha + shock,   data    = dat,   theta   = c(mu = 0.25, nu = 1.10, beta1 = 0, beta2 = 0, beta3 = 0),   dist    = "weibull",   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(maxit = 300) ) summary(fit2) #> hazard model summary #>   observations: 180  #>   predictors:   3  #>   dist:         weibull  #>   engine:       native-r-m2  #>   converged:    TRUE  #>   log-lik:      -293.553  #>   evaluations: fn=36, gr=8 #>  #> Coefficients: #>          estimate   std_error      z_stat      p_value #> mu    0.121860518 0.062260594  1.95726558 5.031625e-02 #> nu    1.143730632 0.084302528 13.56697905 6.285984e-42 #> beta1 0.001716551 0.008807986  0.19488579 8.454824e-01 #> beta2 0.156208724 0.090597558  1.72420457 8.467092e-02 #> beta3 0.017352702 0.362918437  0.04781433 9.618642e-01  # \donttest{ # -- Parametric survival with Kaplan-Meier overlay ----------------- if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(ggplot2)    # Parametric curve on a fine grid at median covariate profile   t_grid   <- seq(0.05, max(dat$time), length.out = 80)   curve_df <- data.frame(     time = t_grid, age = median(dat$age), nyha = 2, shock = 0   )   curve_df$survival <- predict(fit2, newdata = curve_df,                                type = "survival") * 100    # Kaplan-Meier empirical overlay   km    <- survival::survfit(survival::Surv(time, status) ~ 1, data = dat)   km_df <- data.frame(time = km$time, survival = km$surv * 100)    ggplot() +     geom_step(data = km_df, aes(time, survival, colour = "Kaplan-Meier")) +     geom_line(data = curve_df, aes(time, survival,                                    colour = "Parametric (Weibull)")) +     scale_colour_manual(       values = c("Parametric (Weibull)" = "#0072B2",                  "Kaplan-Meier"         = "#D55E00")     ) +     scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +     labs(x = "Months after surgery", y = "Freedom from death (%)",          colour = NULL) +     theme_minimal() +     theme(legend.position = "bottom") }  # }  # \donttest{ # -- Multiphase model (two phases) --------------------------------- fit_mp <- hazard(   survival::Surv(time, status) ~ 1,   data   = dat,   dist   = "multiphase",   phases = list(     early = hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                        fixed = "shapes"),     late  = hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 5,   nu = 1, m = 0,                        fixed = "shapes")   ),   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(n_starts = 5, maxit = 1000) ) summary(fit_mp) #> Multiphase hazard model (2 phases) #>   observations: 180  #>   predictors:   0  #>   dist:         multiphase  #>   phase 1:      early - cdf (early risk) #>   phase 2:      late - cdf (late risk) #>   engine:       native-r-m2  #>   converged:    TRUE  #>   log-lik:      -321.926  #>   evaluations: fn=16, gr=7 #>  #> Coefficients (internal scale): #>  #>   Phase: early (cdf) #>                estimate std_error z_stat p_value #>   log_mu     -1.8209827        NA     NA      NA #>   log_t_half -0.6931472        NA     NA      NA #>   nu          2.0000000        NA     NA      NA #>   m           0.0000000        NA     NA      NA #>  #>   Phase: late (cdf) #>               estimate  std_error   z_stat      p_value #>   log_mu     0.6119202 0.05531211 11.06304 1.895578e-28 #>   log_t_half 1.6094379         NA       NA           NA #>   nu         1.0000000         NA       NA           NA #>   m          0.0000000         NA       NA           NA  # -- Per-phase decomposed cumulative hazard ------------------------ if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   t_grid <- seq(0.01, max(dat$time), length.out = 100)   decomp <- predict(fit_mp, newdata = data.frame(time = t_grid),                     type = "cumulative_hazard", decompose = TRUE)    df_long <- data.frame(     time = rep(decomp$time, 3),     cumhaz = c(decomp$total, decomp$early, decomp$late),     component = rep(c("Total", "Early (cdf)", "Late (cdf)"),                     each = nrow(decomp))   )   df_long$component <- factor(df_long$component,     levels = c("Total", "Early (cdf)", "Late (cdf)"))    ggplot2::ggplot(df_long,     ggplot2::aes(x = time, y = cumhaz, colour = component,                  linewidth = component)) +     ggplot2::geom_line() +     ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c(       "Total" = "black", "Early (cdf)" = "#0072B2",       "Late (cdf)" = "#D55E00"     )) +     ggplot2::scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(       "Total" = 1.2, "Early (cdf)" = 0.6, "Late (cdf)" = 0.6     )) +     ggplot2::labs(       x = "Time", y = "Cumulative hazard H(t)",       colour = NULL, linewidth = NULL,       title = "Multiphase decomposition: early + late"     ) +     ggplot2::theme_minimal() +     ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom") }  # }

what: Bootstrap resampling for hazard model coefficients — hzr_bootstrap
title: Bootstrap resampling for hazard model coefficients — hzr_bootstrap
text: Resample data replacement, refit hazard model replicate, accumulate coefficient distributions. Returns tidy data frame per-replicate estimates summary statistics. R equivalent SAS bootstrap.hazard.sas macro.

title: Bootstrap resampling for hazard model coefficients — hzr_bootstrap
code: hzr_bootstrap(   object,   n_boot = 200L,   fraction = 1,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'hzr_bootstrap' print(x, digits = 4, ...)

title: Bootstrap resampling for hazard model coefficients — hzr_bootstrap
text: object fitted hazard object (fit = TRUE). n_boot Integer: number bootstrap replicates (default 200). fraction Numeric (0, 1]: fraction data sample per replicate (default 1.0 full bootstrap; < 1 bagging). seed Optional integer random seed reproducibility. verbose Logical; TRUE, print progress every 50 replicates. x hzr_bootstrap object. digits Number decimal places formatting. ... Additional arguments (ignored).

title: Bootstrap resampling for hazard model coefficients — hzr_bootstrap
text: list class "hzr_bootstrap" containing: replicates Data frame columns replicate, parameter, estimate – one row per parameter per successful replicate. summary Data frame columns parameter, n, pct, mean, sd, min, max, ci_lower, ci_upper – one row per parameter. n_success Number successfully converged replicates. n_failed Number replicates failed converge.

title: Bootstrap resampling for hazard model coefficients — hzr_bootstrap
code: # \donttest{ data(avc) avc <- na.omit(avc) fit <- hazard(   survival::Surv(int_dead, dead) ~ age + mal,   data  = avc,   dist  = "weibull",   theta = c(mu = 0.01, nu = 0.5, 0, 0),   fit   = TRUE ) bs <- hzr_bootstrap(fit, n_boot = 50, seed = 123) print(bs) #> Bootstrap inference for hazard model #> Replicates: 50 successful, 0 failed #>  #>  parameter  n pct    mean     sd     min     max ci_lower ci_upper #>         mu 50 100  0.0004 0.0004  0.0000  0.0023   0.0000   0.0013 #>         nu 50 100  0.2219 0.0144  0.1963  0.2618   0.1986   0.2462 #>        age 50 100 -0.0062 0.0028 -0.0129 -0.0022  -0.0125  -0.0029 #>        mal 50 100  0.8464 0.2701  0.2673  1.3909   0.4337   1.3471 # }

what: Calibrate a continuous variable against an outcome — hzr_calibrate
title: Calibrate a continuous variable against an outcome — hzr_calibrate
text: Group continuous covariate quantile bins, compute event probability (hazard rate) per bin, apply link transform (logit, Gompertz, Cox). R equivalent SAS logit.sas logitgr.sas macros.

title: Calibrate a continuous variable against an outcome — hzr_calibrate
code: hzr_calibrate(   x,   event,   groups = 10L,   by = NULL,   link = c("logit", "gompertz", "cox"),   time = NULL )

title: Calibrate a continuous variable against an outcome — hzr_calibrate
text: x Numeric vector: continuous covariate calibrate. event Numeric vector: event indicator (1 = event, 0 = event). groups Integer: number quantile bins (default 10). Optional factor character vector stratified calibration (SAS logitgr.sas functionality). provided, calibration computed within stratum. Default NULL (stratification). link Character: transform apply event probabilities. One "logit" (default), "gompertz" (complementary log-log), "cox". time Optional numeric vector: follow-time, required link = "cox". Cox link computes \(\log(\text{events} / \sum \text{time})\) (constant hazard rate).

title: Calibrate a continuous variable against an outcome — hzr_calibrate
text: data frame one row per group (per group--stratum combination) columns: group Integer group label. Stratum level (present provided). n Number observations group. events Number events. mean Mean x within group. min Minimum x within group. max Maximum x within group. prob Event probability (events / n), Cox link: events / sum(time). link_value Transformed probability chosen link scale.

title: Calibrate a continuous variable against an outcome — hzr_calibrate
text: Use function model entry assess whether covariate's relationship outcome approximately linear link scale. transformed probabilities roughly linear group means, covariate can enter model untransformed. Curvature suggests transformation (log, quadratic) may improve fit.

title: Calibrate a continuous variable against an outcome — hzr_calibrate
code: data(avc) avc <- na.omit(avc)  # Logit calibration of age cal <- hzr_calibrate(avc$age, avc$dead, groups = 10) print(cal) #> Variable calibration (logit link, 10 groups) #>  #>  group  n events    mean     min     max  prob link_value #>      1 30     11   3.519   1.051   5.388 0.367     -0.547 #>      2 31     11   8.665   5.421  11.532 0.355     -0.598 #>      3 30     13  15.194  11.631  18.497 0.433     -0.268 #>      4 31     11  23.077  18.990  27.828 0.355     -0.598 #>      5 30      7  43.544  28.124  57.167 0.233     -1.190 #>      6 31      3  72.066  59.730  86.408 0.097     -2.234 #>      7 30      2 101.154  86.507 117.522 0.067     -2.639 #>      8 31      3 162.739 121.169 203.733 0.097     -2.234 #>      9 30      4 247.051 205.343 297.140 0.133     -1.872 #>     10 31      3 530.623 324.573 790.981 0.097     -2.234  # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(ggplot2)   ggplot(cal, aes(mean, link_value)) +     geom_point(size = 3) +     geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, se = FALSE,                 linetype = "dashed") +     labs(x = "Age at repair (months)", y = "Logit(P(death))") +     theme_minimal() }  # }

Competing risks cumulative incidence — hzr_competing_risks
text: Compute cumulative incidence functions multiple competing event types using Aalen-Johansen estimator Greenwood variance. R equivalent SAS markov.sas macro.

title: Competing risks cumulative incidence — hzr_competing_risks
code: hzr_competing_risks(time, event)  # S3 method for class 'hzr_competing_risks' print(x, digits = 4, ...)

title: Competing risks cumulative incidence — hzr_competing_risks
text: time Numeric vector follow-times. event Integer vector event type indicators: 0 = censored, 1 = event type 1, 2 = event type 2, etc. x hzr_competing_risks object. digits Number decimal places formatting. ... Additional arguments (ignored).

title: Competing risks cumulative incidence — hzr_competing_risks
text: data frame one row per unique event time columns: time Event time. n_risk Number risk. n_event_1, n_event_2, ... Events type time. n_censor Number censored time. surv Overall event-free survival (freedom events). incid_1, incid_2, ... Cumulative incidence event type. se_surv Standard error overall survival. se_1, se_2, ... Standard error cumulative incidence.

title: Competing risks cumulative incidence — hzr_competing_risks
text: Unlike naive 1 - KM estimator (overestimates incidence competing risks exist), provides correct marginal cumulative incidence event type.

title: Competing risks cumulative incidence — hzr_competing_risks
code: data(valves) valves_cc <- na.omit(valves) # Combine death and PVE into a competing risks event variable # 0 = censored, 1 = death, 2 = PVE event_cr <- ifelse(valves_cc$dead == 1, 1L,                    ifelse(valves_cc$pve == 1, 2L, 0L)) time_cr <- pmin(valves_cc$int_dead, valves_cc$int_pve) cr <- hzr_competing_risks(time_cr, event_cr) head(cr) #> Competing risks cumulative incidence #> Event types: 2  | Time points: 6  #> Final survival: 0.9888  #> Final incid_1 : 0.0112  #> Final incid_2 : 0  #>  #>     time n_risk n_event_1 n_event_2 n_censor   surv incid_1 incid_2 se_surv #>  0.00068   1523         1         0        0 0.9993  0.0007       0  0.0007 #>  0.00137   1522         4         0        0 0.9967  0.0033       0  0.0015 #>  0.00171   1518         1         0        0 0.9961  0.0039       0  0.0016 #>  0.00205   1517         3         0        0 0.9941  0.0059       0  0.0020 #>  0.00274   1514         4         0        0 0.9915  0.0085       0  0.0024 #>  0.00411   1510         4         0        0 0.9888  0.0112       0  0.0027 #>    se_1 se_2 #>  0.0007    0 #>  0.0015    0 #>  0.0016    0 #>  0.0020    0 #>  0.0024    0 #>  0.0027    0

title: Decile-of-risk calibration — hzr_deciles
text: Partition observations groups (default 10) predicted risk compare observed vs. expected event counts group.  Good calibration means two track across risk spectrum.

title: Decile-of-risk calibration — hzr_deciles
code: hzr_deciles(object, time, groups = 10L, status = NULL, event_time = NULL)

title: Decile-of-risk calibration — hzr_deciles
text: object fitted hazard object (fit = TRUE). time Numeric scalar: time point evaluate predicted survival / cumulative hazard.  example, time = 12 evaluates 12-month predictions. groups Integer: number risk groups (default 10 deciles). status Optional numeric vector event indicators (1 = event, 0 = censored). NULL (default), extracted fitted object's stored data. event_time Optional numeric vector observed event/censoring times.  NULL (default), extracted fitted object.

title: Decile-of-risk calibration — hzr_deciles
text: data frame one row per risk group columns: group Integer group label (1 = lowest risk). n Number observations group. events Observed event count requested horizon (event indicator = 1 event time <= time). expected Expected event count requested horizon, computed sum individual event probabilities (\(1 - S(time)\)). observed_rate Observed event rate (events / n). expected_rate Expected event rate (expected / n). chi_sq Chi-square contribution: (events - expected)^2 / expected. p_value Upper-tail p-value chi-square test group (1 df). mean_survival Mean predicted survival probability group. mean_cumhaz Mean predicted cumulative hazard group. attribute "overall" attached overall chi-square statistic, degrees freedom, p-value.

title: Decile-of-risk calibration — hzr_deciles
text: implements workflow SAS deciles.hazard.sas macro. Patients ranked predicted cumulative hazard specified time point, grouped quantile bins, bin tested chi-square goodness--fit statistic. Subjects censored requested horizon excluded observed-vs-expected comparison.

title: Decile-of-risk calibration — hzr_deciles
Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: Computes cumulative distribution \(G(t)\), density \(g(t)\), hazard \(h(t) = g(t)/(1 - G(t))\) parametric family defined half-life, time exponent, shape.  single function generates temporal phase shapes used multiphase hazard models.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
code: hzr_decompos(time, t_half, nu, m)

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: time Numeric vector times (must > 0). t_half Half-life: time \(G(t_{1/2}) = 0.5\). Must > 0. nu Time exponent controlling rate dynamics. SAS early: NU.  SAS late: relates GAMMA/ETA. m Shape exponent controlling distributional form. SAS early: M.  SAS late: relates GAMMA/ALPHA.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: named list three numeric vectors, length time: G Cumulative distribution \(G(t) \[0, 1]\). g Density \(g(t) = dG/dt \ge 0\).  "early" phase temporal pattern. h Hazard \(h(t) = g(t)/(1 - G(t)) \ge 0\).  "late" phase temporal pattern.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: original C code used separate parameterizations early (DELTA, RHO/THALF, NU, M) late (TAU, GAMMA, ALPHA, ETA) phases.  collapse onto three parameters .  See hzr_argument_mapping() full translation table.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: Six cases defined signs nu m: combination m < 0 nu < 0 undefined raises error.

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
text: Blackstone EH, Naftel DC, Turner Jr. decomposition time-varying hazard phases, incorporating separate stream concomitant information. J Stat Assoc. 1986;81(395):615–624. doi:10.1080/01621459.1986.10478314 Rajeswaran J, Blackstone EH, Ehrlinger J, Li L, Ishwaran H, Parides MK. Probability atrial fibrillation ablation: Using parametric nonlinear temporal decomposition mixed effects model. Stat Methods Med Res. 2018;27(1):126–141. doi:10.1177/0962280215623583

title: Generalized temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1)  # Case 1: standard sigmoidal (m > 0, nu > 0) d1 <- hzr_decompos(t_grid, t_half = 3, nu = 2, m = 1) plot(t_grid, d1$G, type = "l", main = "CDF (m=1, nu=2)")   # Case 1L: Weibull-like (m = 0, nu > 0) d1L <- hzr_decompos(t_grid, t_half = 3, nu = 2, m = 0)  # Case 2: heavy-tailed (m < 0, nu > 0) d2 <- hzr_decompos(t_grid, t_half = 3, nu = 2, m = -1)

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: Computes cumulative intensity \(G_3(t)\) derivative \(g_3(t) = dG_3/dt\) late-phase parametric family used original Blackstone C/SAS HAZARD code.  Unlike hzr_decompos() (computes early-phase G1 – bounded CDF), function can produce unbounded values, making suitable modelling increasing late risk.

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
code: hzr_decompos_g3(time, tau, gamma, alpha, eta)

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: time Numeric vector times (must > 0). tau Positive scalar scale parameter. gamma Positive scalar time exponent. alpha Non-negative scalar shape parameter (0 selects limiting case). eta Positive scalar outer exponent.

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: named list two numeric vectors, length time: G3 Cumulative intensity \(G_3(t) \ge 0\) (may exceed 1). g3 Derivative \(g_3(t) = dG_3/dt \ge 0\).

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: \(\alpha > 0\): $$G_3(t) = \bigl(\bigl((t/\tau)^\gamma + 1\bigr)^{1/\alpha}                       - 1\bigr)^\eta$$ \(\alpha = 0\) (limiting exponential case): $$G_3(t) = \bigl(\exp\bigl((t/\tau)^\gamma\bigr) - 1\bigr)^\eta$$

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
text: Blackstone EH, Naftel DC, Turner Jr. decomposition time-varying hazard phases, incorporating separate stream concomitant information. J Stat Assoc. 1986;81(395):615–624. doi:10.1080/01621459.1986.10478314

title: Late-phase (G3) temporal decomposition — hzr_decompos_g3
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1)  # Weibull-like: alpha = 1 gives G3(t) = (t/tau)^(gamma*eta) d <- hzr_decompos_g3(t_grid, tau = 1, gamma = 3, alpha = 1, eta = 1) plot(t_grid, d$G3, type = "l", main = "G3: power law (gamma=3)")   # General case with alpha > 0 d2 <- hzr_decompos_g3(t_grid, tau = 2, gamma = 2, alpha = 0.5, eta = 1)

what: Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. predicted events — hzr_gof
title: Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. predicted events — hzr_gof
text: Compare fitted hazard model nonparametric Kaplan-Meier estimate computing observed expected (parametric) event counts distinct event time.  R equivalent SAS hazplot.sas macro implements conservation--events diagnostic.

title: Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. predicted events — hzr_gof
code: hzr_gof(object, time_grid = NULL)

title: Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. predicted events — hzr_gof
text: object fitted hazard object (fit = TRUE). time_grid Optional numeric vector time points evaluate parametric model. NULL (default), uses sorted unique event times fitted data.

title: Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. predicted events — hzr_gof
text: data frame one row per time point columns: time Evaluation time. n_risk Number risk (Kaplan-Meier). n_event Number events time. n_censor Number censored time. km_surv Kaplan-Meier survival estimate. km_cumhaz Kaplan-Meier cumulative hazard (\(-\log(\text{km\_surv})\)). par_surv Parametric survival fitted model. par_cumhaz Parametric cumulative hazard. cum_observed Cumulative observed events time. cum_expected Cumulative expected events (sum individual cumulative hazards observations exiting risk set). residual Expected minus observed (cum_expected - cum_observed). multiphase models, additional columns appended phase: par_cumhaz_<phase>. attribute "summary" attached scalar diagnostics: total observed events, total expected events, final residual.

title: Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. predicted events — hzr_gof
text: observed event time function computes: Kaplan-Meier survival cumulative hazard. parametric survival cumulative hazard fitted model (per-phase components multiphase models). Cumulative observed events vs. cumulative expected events (sum individual cumulative hazards exiting risk set time). running residual (expected minus observed). Perfect model fit implies expected observed event counts track (residual near zero).  conservation--events principle.

title: Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. predicted events — hzr_gof
code: # \donttest{ data(avc) avc <- na.omit(avc) fit <- hazard(   survival::Surv(int_dead, dead) ~ age + mal,   data  = avc,   dist  = "weibull",   theta = c(mu = 0.01, nu = 0.5, beta_age = 0, beta_mal = 0),   fit   = TRUE ) gof <- hzr_gof(fit) print(gof) #> Goodness-of-fit: observed vs. expected events #> Distribution: weibull  | n = 305  #>  #> Total observed events: 68  #> Total expected events: 55.9  #> Final residual (E - O): -12.1  #> Conservation ratio (E/O): 0.822  #>  #> Use plot columns: time, km_surv, par_surv, cum_observed, cum_expected, residual  # Plot observed vs expected events if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(ggplot2)   ggplot(gof, aes(x = time)) +     geom_line(aes(y = cum_observed), colour = "#D55E00") +     geom_line(aes(y = cum_expected), colour = "#0072B2") +     labs(x = "Time", y = "Cumulative events") +     theme_minimal() }  # }

what: Kaplan-Meier survival with exact logit confidence limits — hzr_kaplan
title: Kaplan-Meier survival with exact logit confidence limits — hzr_kaplan
text: Compute product-limit (Kaplan-Meier) survival estimate logit-transformed confidence limits respect \([0, 1]\) boundary. R equivalent SAS kaplan.sas macro.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival with exact logit confidence limits — hzr_kaplan
code: hzr_kaplan(time, status, conf_level = 0.95, event_only = TRUE)

title: Kaplan-Meier survival with exact logit confidence limits — hzr_kaplan
text: time Numeric vector follow-times. status Numeric event indicator (1 = event, 0 = censored). conf_level Confidence level interval (default 0.95). SAS default 0.68268948 corresponds 1-SD interval. event_only Logical; TRUE (default), return rows event times (n_event > 0). FALSE, return rows times reported survival::survfit() (events censoring times).

title: Kaplan-Meier survival with exact logit confidence limits — hzr_kaplan
text: data frame one row per time point columns: time Event/censoring time. n_risk Number risk start interval. n_event Number events time. n_censor Number censored time. survival Kaplan-Meier survival estimate. std_err Standard error survival (Greenwood). cl_lower Lower confidence limit (logit-transformed). cl_upper Upper confidence limit (logit-transformed). cumhaz Cumulative hazard \(= -\log(S)\). hazard Interval hazard rate \(= \log(S_{t-1} / S_t) / \Delta t\). density Probability density estimate \(= (S_{t-1} - S_t) / \Delta t\). life Restricted mean survival time (area curve time).

title: Kaplan-Meier survival with exact logit confidence limits — hzr_kaplan
text: standard Greenwood confidence interval can exceed \([0, 1]\) tails. logit-transformed interval avoids working log-odds scale: $$   \text{CL}_{\text{lower}} = S / \bigl(S + (1-S)\,   \exp(z_\alpha\,\text{SI})\bigr) $$ $$   \text{CL}_{\text{upper}} = S / \bigl(S + (1-S)\,   \exp(-z_\alpha\,\text{SI})\bigr) $$ \(\text{SI} = \sqrt{V_P - 1} / (1 - S)\), \(V_P\) cumulative Greenwood variance product, \(z_\alpha\) normal quantile requested confidence level.

what: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: Creates hzr_phase object describing one term multiphase additive cumulative hazard model.  Pass list phases argument hazard() dist = "multiphase".

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
code: hzr_phase(   type = c("cdf", "hazard", "constant", "g3"),   t_half = 1,   nu = 1,   m = 0,   tau = 1,   gamma = 1,   alpha = 1,   eta = 1,   formula = NULL,   fixed = character(0) )  # S3 method for class 'hzr_phase' print(x, ...)

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: type Character; phase type: "cdf", "hazard", "g3", "constant". t_half Positive scalar; initial half-life (time \(G(t_{1/2}) = 0.5\)).  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases. SAS early: THALF/RHO. nu Numeric scalar; initial time exponent.  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases.  SAS early: NU. m Numeric scalar; initial shape exponent.  Used "cdf" "hazard" phases.  SAS early: M. tau Positive scalar; scale parameter "g3" phases. SAS late: TAU. gamma Positive scalar; time exponent "g3" phases. SAS late: GAMMA. alpha Non-negative scalar; shape parameter "g3" phases. alpha > 0, generic G3 formula used; alpha = 0 gives exponential limiting case.  SAS late: ALPHA. eta Positive scalar; outer exponent "g3" phases. SAS late: ETA. formula Optional one-sided formula (e.g. ~ age + nyha) phase-specific covariates.  NULL (default), phase inherits global formula hazard(). fixed Character vector naming shape parameters hold fixed optimization.  Valid names "cdf"/"hazard": "t_half", "nu", "m", "shapes" (shorthand three).  Valid names "g3": "tau", "gamma", "alpha", "eta", "shapes" (shorthand four).  Fixed parameters held starting values; mu (covariates) estimated.  Ignored "constant" phases. mirrors SAS/C HAZARD workflow shapes typically fixed scale parameters estimated. x hzr_phase object (print.hzr_phase()). ... Additional arguments (ignored).

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: S3 object class "hzr_phase" elements: type Phase type string. t_half Initial half-life (cdf/hazard phases). nu Initial time exponent (cdf/hazard phases). m Initial shape exponent (cdf/hazard phases). tau Scale parameter (g3 phases). gamma Time exponent (g3 phases). alpha Shape parameter (g3 phases). eta Outer exponent (g3 phases). formula Phase-specific formula NULL. fixed Character vector fixed parameter names (may empty).

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
text: "cdf" Early risk resolves time. \(\Phi(t) = G(t)\), bounded \([0, 1]\). SAS equivalent: Early / G1 phase. "hazard" Late aging risk accumulates. \(\Phi(t) = -\log(1 - G(t))\), monotone increasing. SAS equivalent: Late / G3 phase. "constant" Flat background hazard rate. \(\Phi(t) = t\). shape parameters estimated. SAS equivalent: Constant / G2 phase.

title: Specify a single hazard phase — hzr_phase
code: # Classic 3-phase Blackstone pattern early <- hzr_phase("cdf",      t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0) const <- hzr_phase("constant") late  <- hzr_phase("g3", tau = 1, gamma = 3, alpha = 1, eta = 1)  # Fix all shapes (C/SAS-style: only estimate mu) early_fixed <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                           fixed = "shapes") late_fixed  <- hzr_phase("g3", tau = 1, gamma = 3, alpha = 1, eta = 1,                           fixed = "shapes")  # Fix only some parameters early_partial <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                             fixed = c("nu", "m"))  # Phase with specific covariates early_cov <- hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                         formula = ~ age + shock)  # Use in hazard(): # hazard(Surv(time, status) ~ age, data = dat, #        dist = "multiphase", #        phases = list(early = early, constant = const, late = late))

what: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: Computes \(\Phi_j(t)\) one phase additive model \(H(t|x) = \sum_j \mu_j(x) \Phi_j(t)\).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
code: hzr_phase_cumhaz(   time,   t_half = 1,   nu = 1,   m = 0,   type = c("cdf", "hazard", "constant") )

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: time Numeric vector times (> 0). t_half Half-life parameter (> 0). nu Time exponent. m Shape parameter. type Phase type: "cdf" (early – uses \(G(t)\)), "hazard" (late – uses cumulative hazard \(h(t)\)), "constant" (flat rate – \(\Phi = t\)).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: Numeric vector cumulative hazard contributions \(\Phi(t)\), length time.

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
text: "cdf": \(\Phi(t) = G(t)\).  Bounded \([0, 1]\).  Models early risk resolves time. "hazard": \(\Phi(t) = -\log(1 - G(t))\).  Monotone increasing. Models late aging risk.  cumulative hazard derived hazard function \(h(t)\), since \(\int_0^t h(s)\,ds = -\log(1 - G(t))\). "constant": \(\Phi(t) = t\).  Ignores t_half, nu, m. Equivalent exponential (constant hazard rate).

title: Cumulative hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_cumhaz
code: t_grid <- seq(0.1, 10, by = 0.1) phi_early <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, t_half = 2, nu = 2, m = 0,                                type = "cdf") phi_late  <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, t_half = 5, nu = 1, m = 0,                                type = "hazard") phi_const <- hzr_phase_cumhaz(t_grid, type = "constant")

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: time Numeric vector times (> 0). t_half Half-life parameter (> 0). nu Time exponent. m Shape parameter. type Phase type: "cdf" (early – uses \(G(t)\)), "hazard" (late – uses cumulative hazard \(h(t)\)), "constant" (flat rate – \(\Phi = t\)).

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: Numeric vector instantaneous hazard contributions \(\phi(t)\), length time.

title: Instantaneous hazard contribution from a single phase — hzr_phase_hazard
text: "cdf": \(\phi(t) = g(t)\) (density). "hazard": \(\phi(t) = h(t) = g(t)/(1-G(t))\). "constant": \(\phi(t) = 1\).

what: Stepwise covariate selection for a parametric hazard model — hzr_stepwise
title: Stepwise covariate selection for a parametric hazard model — hzr_stepwise
text: Run forward, backward, two-way stepwise selection existing hazard fit using Wald p-values AIC deltas entry / retention criterion.  Phase-specific entry supported multiphase models: covariate can enter one phase another.

title: Stepwise covariate selection for a parametric hazard model — hzr_stepwise
code: hzr_stepwise(   fit,   scope = NULL,   data,   direction = c("both", "forward", "backward"),   criterion = c("wald", "aic"),   slentry = 0.3,   slstay = 0.2,   max_steps = 50L,   max_move = 4L,   force_in = character(),   force_out = character(),   trace = TRUE,   ... )

title: Stepwise covariate selection for a parametric hazard model — hzr_stepwise
text: fit fitted hazard object built via formula = Surv(...) ~ predictors, data = df interface. scope Candidate set.  NULL (default) uses every data-frame column already model every phase.  single-distribution fits, pass one-sided formula (~ age + nyha) character vector names.  multiphase fits, pass named list one-sided formulas keyed phase. data Data frame base fit built .  Required refits. direction One "" (default), "forward", "backward". criterion One "wald" (default) "aic".  SAS-style p-value thresholds apply Wald; AIC uses DeltaAIC < 0 uniformly. slentry Entry p-value threshold Wald criterion. Default 0.30 matches SAS SLENTRY. slstay Retention p-value threshold Wald criterion. Default 0.20 matches SAS SLSTAY. max_steps Hard cap total accepted actions.  Emits warning() hit.  Default 50. max_move Per-variable oscillation cap.  variable entered + exited max_move times frozen remainder run.  Default 4. force_in Character vector variables must remain model.  variables still scored reported selection trace, never dropped. force_out Character vector variables may never considered candidates. trace Logical; print step--step progress console. Default TRUE. ... Passed underlying hazard() refits (e.g. control = list(n_starts = 3)).

title: Stepwise covariate selection for a parametric hazard model — hzr_stepwise
text: object class c("hzr_stepwise", "hazard") – final fit augmented : steps Data frame one row per accepted / frozen action; see Details. scope Record candidate scope, plus force_in, force_out, frozen set. criteria Named list threshold / direction settings actually applied. trace_msg Character vector trace lines, captured regardless trace flag. elapsed difftime start finish. final_call call produced result.

title: Stepwise covariate selection for a parametric hazard model — hzr_stepwise
text: steps data frame columns: step_num Integer sequence starting 1. action "enter", "drop", "frozen". variable Variable affected. phase Phase name (multiphase) NA_character_. criterion "wald" "aic". score Winning score used decision. stat, df Wald statistic degrees freedom. p_value, delta_aic Always populated computable, regardless active criterion. logLik, aic, n_coef Goodness--fit diagnostics model step.

title: Summarize a hazard model — summary.hazard
code: # -- Single-phase Weibull summary ------------------------------------ fit <- hazard(time = rexp(30, 0.5), status = rep(1L, 30),               theta = c(0.3, 1.0), dist = "weibull", fit = TRUE) summary(fit) #> hazard model summary #>   observations: 30  #>   predictors:   0  #>   dist:         weibull  #>   engine:       native-r-m2  #>   converged:    TRUE  #>   log-lik:      -55.1318  #>   evaluations: fn=31, gr=5 #>  #> Coefficients: #>     estimate std_error   z_stat      p_value #> mu 0.4582700 0.1066703 4.296136 1.738007e-05 #> nu 0.8182644 0.1251257 6.539537 6.170969e-11  # \donttest{ # -- Multiphase model summary ---------------------------------------- set.seed(42) n   <- 200 dat <- data.frame(   time   = rexp(n, rate = 0.25) + 0.01,   status = rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.65) ) fit_mp <- hazard(   survival::Surv(time, status) ~ 1,   data   = dat,   dist   = "multiphase",   phases = list(     early = hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 0.5, nu = 2, m = 0,                        fixed = "shapes"),     late  = hzr_phase("cdf", t_half = 5,   nu = 1, m = 0,                        fixed = "shapes")   ),   fit     = TRUE,   control = list(n_starts = 5, maxit = 1000) ) summary(fit_mp) #> Multiphase hazard model (2 phases) #>   observations: 200  #>   predictors:   0  #>   dist:         multiphase  #>   phase 1:      early - cdf (early risk) #>   phase 2:      late - cdf (late risk) #>   engine:       native-r-m2  #>   converged:    TRUE  #>   log-lik:      -428.716  #>   evaluations: fn=26, gr=5 #>  #> Coefficients (internal scale): #>  #>   Phase: early (cdf) #>                estimate std_error z_stat p_value #>   log_mu     -2.1153072        NA     NA      NA #>   log_t_half -0.6931472        NA     NA      NA #>   nu          2.0000000        NA     NA      NA #>   m           0.0000000        NA     NA      NA #>  #>   Phase: late (cdf) #>               estimate  std_error  z_stat      p_value #>   log_mu     0.5511641 0.04098849 13.4468 3.214692e-41 #>   log_t_half 1.6094379         NA      NA           NA #>   nu         1.0000000         NA      NA           NA #>   m          0.0000000         NA      NA           NA # }

TemporalHazard 0.9.7
text: Counting-process / repeating-events likelihood wired — Phase 4f development plan lands. Surv(start, stop, event) start > 0 now accepted. Weibull multiphase log-likelihoods apply H(stop) - H(start) event right-censored terms; trivial start = 0 case degenerates H(stop) recovers plain-Surv fit exactly. Splitting row contiguous epochs preserves log-likelihood MLE optimizer tolerance (split-invariance). Weibull + multiphase analytic gradients handle H(start). closed-form Weibull score adds -d H(start)/d theta term per row (guarded start = 0). multiphase analytic gradient computes per-phase Phi_j(start) shape derivatives, adds +w_H_start * mu_j * dPhi_j(start) parameter’s score; G3 phase derivatives start use finite-difference machinery stop. 0.9.5 narrowing removed. hazard() guard rejected counting-process Surv(start, stop, event) start > 0 gone.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.6
text: weights now supported distributions — Phase 4e development plan lands. exponential, log-logistic, log-normal likelihoods analytic gradients now apply row weights every censoring term (event, right-censored, left-censored, interval-censored). 0.9.5 guard hazard() rejected weights dist %% c("exponential",   "loglogistic", "lognormal") removed. Fits integer weights reproduce row-duplicated fit optimizer tolerance across five distributions. Conservation Events now honours weights. .hzr_conserve_events() .hzr_select_fixmu_phase() take optional weights argument; multiphase optimizer threads per-phase cumulative hazards summed scale (weighted) observed event count. CoE longer auto-disables weights non-uniform — dimension reduction stays MLE matches full-dim path.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.6
text: Multiphase analytic gradient now applies weights. .hzr_gradient_multiphase() accepted neither weights downstream equivalents: per-row score weights w_H / inv_h set ±1 interval-censored finite-difference correction summed unweighted LL. Weighted multiphase fits therefore optimised weighted objective unweighted score; BFGS line search still converged near correct MLE final gradient norm go zero. three paths now honour row weights, optimizer’s gradient_fn wrapper (including -zero numeric fallback CoE wrapper) forwards weights consistently. Regression test covers weighted analytic vs numerical gradient parity. Surfaced Copilot review PR #18.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.5
text: Stepwise covariate selection — hzr_stepwise() runs forward, backward, two-way stepwise selection existing hazard fit using Wald p-values AIC deltas entry / retention criterion. Phase-specific entry supported multiphase models: covariate can enter one phase another. Defaults match SAS PROC HAZARD (SLENTRY = 0.30, SLSTAY = 0.20); AIC mode uses ΔAIC < 0 uniformly. SAS-style MOVE oscillation guard freezes variables enter + exit max_move times. Returns object class c("hzr_stepwise", "hazard") $steps selection trace, scope record, elapsed timer. Implements core algorithm C HAZARD stepw.c / backw.c.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.5
text: Multiphase convergence weights/repeating-events merge — restored multiphase optimization regressed 0.9.4: three interacting defects new weights threading (dup-arg collision multiphase / Weibull closures, positional-arg corruption every distribution’s gradient call) made every optimizer iteration error silently inside tryCatch. Diagnosed fixed via commit 73b4657. Weibull analytic gradient now applies weights — .hzr_gradient_weibull() grad_internal closure inside .hzr_optim_weibull() accepted weights formal apply score vector. optimizer still converged via line search (weighted) log-likelihood, gradient direction wrong final gradient norm go zero. gradient paths now weight event indicator cumulative hazard building blocks. Fits integer weights reproduce equivalent row-duplicated fit optimizer tolerance.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.5
text: weights now accepted dist = "weibull" dist = "multiphase". 0.9.4 NEWS claimed weights threaded distribution-specific likelihoods; fact exponential, log-logistic, log-normal single-distribution paths accepted formal never applied , fit silently unweighted. hazard() now raises explicit error weights supplied one distributions rather returning unweighted fit. Full support remaining single-dist paths tracked inst/dev/DEVELOPMENT-PLAN.md Phase 4e. Conservation Events auto-disabled weights 1. .hzr_conserve_events() receives weighted event count target sums per-phase cumulative hazards across rows without applying weights, Turner’s adjustment comes mismatched scale. multiphase optimizer now detects non-unit weights skips CoE dimension reduction, falling (correctly weighted) full-dimensional path. Fits still correct; just don’t benefit one-parameter analytical closed-form solve. Weighted CoE wire-tracked alongside weights completion work inst/dev/DEVELOPMENT-PLAN.md Phase 4e. Repeating-events / counting-process notation narrowed. Surv(start, stop, event) start > 0 longer accepted hazard(). 0.9.4 NEWS claimed epoch contributed H(stop) - H(start) likelihood, downstream likelihoods read time_lower interval-censored rows (status == 2); counting-process rows (status {0, 1}) silently scored H(stop) alone, fit nonzero entry times silently wrong. hazard() now raises explicit error. trivial case Surv(0, t, d) – equivalent Surv(t, d) – continues work. Full wire-H(stop) - H(start) distribution paths tracked inst/dev/DEVELOPMENT-PLAN.md Phase 4f.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.4
text: Observation weights — weights argument hazard() applies Fisher weighting log-likelihood dist = "weibull" dist = "multiphase". observation’s contribution multiplied weight, enabling severity-weighted event analyses. Implements SAS WEIGHT statement. original 0.9.4 entry claimed coverage distribution paths; 0.9.5 patch corrected claim fixed gradient wire-bug Weibull path. Repeating events — Surv(start, stop, event) start-stop notation parsed. original 0.9.4 entry claimed epoch contributed H(stop) - H(start) likelihood, downstream likelihoods never applied lower bound counting-process rows; 0.9.5 patch narrowed feature trivial start = 0 case added explicit error nonzero starts.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.3
text: hzr_deciles() — Decile--risk calibration function comparing observed vs. expected event counts across risk groups chi-square GOF testing. Implements SAS deciles.hazard.sas macro workflow. hzr_gof() — Goodness--fit function comparing parametric predictions nonparametric (Kaplan-Meier) estimates observed vs. expected event counting. Implements SAS hazplot.sas macro workflow. hzr_kaplan() — Kaplan-Meier survival estimator logit-transformed confidence limits respect [0, 1] boundary, interval hazard rate, density, restricted mean survival time (life integral). Implements SAS kaplan.sas macro output structure. hzr_calibrate() — Variable calibration function assessing functional form model entry. Groups continuous covariate quantile bins applies logit, Gompertz, Cox link transforms. Supports stratification via parameter. Implements SAS logit.sas logitgr.sas macros. hzr_nelson() — Wayne Nelson cumulative hazard estimator lognormal confidence limits. Supports weighted events severity-adjusted repeated event analyses. Implements SAS nelsonl.sas macro. hzr_bootstrap() — Bootstrap resampling hazard model coefficients bagging support (fractional sampling). Returns per-replicate estimates summary statistics (mean, SD, percentile CI). Implements SAS bootstrap.hazard.sas macro workflow. hzr_competing_risks() — Competing risks cumulative incidence using Aalen-Johansen estimator Greenwood variance. Handles number competing event types. Implements SAS markov.sas macro. Conservation Events (CoE) — Turner’s theorem now integrated multiphase optimizer. One phase’s log_mu scaling parameter solved analytically iteration, reducing optimization dimension 1 improving numerical stability convergence. Enabled default; disable control = list(conserve = FALSE). Implements core algorithm C HAZARD setcoe.c / consrv.c. New vignette: “Complete Clinical Analysis Walkthrough” — end--end workflow Kaplan-Meier baseline validated multivariable model, mirroring SAS HAZARD analytical sequence.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.3
text: Multi-start optimizer now respects user-set RNG seeds reproducibility (removed set.seed(NULL) actively breaking determinism). Vignette metadata normalized YAML vignette: key across 8 files. fit parameter documentation corrected state default FALSE. README now includes key capabilities table development plan link.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.1
text: G3 late-phase decomposition (hzr_phase("g3", ...)) now fully integrated multiphase optimizer, Hessian, prediction pipeline. fixed = "shapes" parameter hzr_phase() allows fixing shape parameters estimation (matching C/SAS HAZARD workflow estimating log-mu scale parameters).

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.1
text: summary.hazard() now correctly reports standard errors parameters fixed. Previously, anyNA(vcov) rejected entire variance-covariance matrix fixed parameters NA entries. print.summary.hazard() coefficient table now shows correct label G3 phases (printing empty parentheses). print.summary.hazard() phase listing now uses phase name CDF labels (e.g., “cdf (late risk)”) instead hardcoded “early risk”. SAS missing value markers (.) CSV datasets now handled via na.strings = c("NA", ".") data-raw/make_data.R, preventing numeric columns read character.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.1
text: Seven Quarto vignettes: getting-started, fitting-hazard-models, prediction-visualization, inference-diagnostics, mathematical-foundations, package-architecture, sas--r-migration. Roxygen examples now include single-phase multiphase models. README switched self-contained CABGKUL examples G3 late phase. Dataset axis labels corrected “Months” (“Years”).

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.1
text: CI workflows updated use roxygen2::load_pkgload lazy data compatibility. Added lintr CI workflow .lintr configuration. pkgdown action bumped peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4. Added use-public-rspm: true CI workflows. Added lintr Suggests.

title: TemporalHazard 0.9.0
text: Multiphase engine: N-phase additive cumulative hazard models via dist = "multiphase" hzr_phase() specification. hzr_decompos() parametric family implementing three-parameter temporal decomposition Blackstone, Naftel, Turner (1986). Multi-start optimizer Hessian-based variance-covariance estimation. C binary parity tests KUL CABG reference dataset. Five clinical reference datasets: avc, cabgkul, omc, tga, valves.

title: TemporalHazard 0.1.0
